title: The Logic of Comparative Advantage
intro: 为什么国家之间要贸易，而不是什么都自己生产？比较优势给出了答案。
paragraphs: Why do countries trade with one another instead of producing everything themselves? The principle of comparative advantage offers a clear answer. Even if one nation can produce every good more efficiently than another, both can still gain by specializing in what they do relatively best and exchanging the surplus.

A country with limited resources should concentrate on the industries where its productivity is highest, and import goods that others can make at lower cost. This division of labor raises total output and lowers prices for consumers everywhere.

Critics argue that free trade can threaten domestic jobs and widen inequality, and these concerns deserve serious attention. Yet most economists agree that, on balance, open markets accelerate growth and spread innovation across borders.
The challenge for policymakers is not whether to trade, but how to distribute its gains fairly and cushion those who are displaced.
cn: 为什么各国要彼此贸易，而不是什么都自己生产？比较优势原理给出了清晰的答案。即使一个国家生产每一种商品都比另一国更高效，双方仍然可以通过各自专注于自己相对最擅长的领域、并交换剩余产品而获益。
资源有限的国家应当集中发展生产率最高的产业，并从其他国家进口那些别人能以更低成本生产的商品。这种分工提高了总产出，也让各地消费者享受到更低的价格。
批评者认为，自由贸易可能威胁国内就业、加剧不平等，这些担忧值得认真对待。但多数经济学家一致认为，总体而言，开放的市场能加速增长，并把创新传播到世界各地。
政策制定者面临的挑战，不是要不要贸易，而是如何公平地分配贸易带来的收益，并缓解那些被取代者所受的冲击。

glossary.def: v. 专门从事（specialize 的现在分词）
glossary.ex: specializing in what they do best
to cushion those who are displaced

glossary.def: adj. 被取代的，失业流离的
